name: SKY130 Analog ODE Solver — Lorenz Attractor
description: Continuous-time analog computer that solves the Lorenz system of differential equations using SKY130 transistor-level circuits. Outputs chaotic attractors in real-time.

lorenz_parameters.note: Classic chaotic regime. Coefficients implemented via gm ratios.

signal_mapping.description: State variables x, y, z mapped to differential voltages around VCM=0.9V
signal_mapping.note: x,y,z each represented as V+ - V- centered at VCM, swing +/-300mV

gm-cell.description: Programmable-gain OTA for coefficient scaling (sigma, rho, beta, unity)

integrator.description: Gm-C integrator with reset — the d/dt operator

multiplier.description: Gilbert cell analog multiplier for x*y and x*z cross-terms

lorenz-core.description: Three coupled ODE channels wired as the Lorenz system

integration.description: Full system with bias generation, output buffers, and end-to-end Lorenz validation

lorenz_trajectory_correlation.description: Cross-correlation between analog output and ideal RK4 reference over first 5 Lyapunov times

attractor_topology.description: Phase portrait must show the characteristic butterfly shape with two distinct lobes

lyapunov_exponent_sign.description: Largest Lyapunov exponent must be positive, confirming chaotic dynamics

total_power_mw.description: Total power consumption during active computation

time_scale_factor.description: Ratio of real time to Lorenz time — analog runs at least 1000x faster than unit-time
